patientSummary: 30 years old Female patient, Mara Sickler was seen on 08-06-2024 as a follow up patient.

complaints:
Laser Hair Treatment
Patient presents for laser hair removal to face. Today is patient's 6th  treatment. 
Patient states to notice improvement and denies skin irritation or burning. Patient denies history of skin cancer, active herpes infection, history of keloid or hypertrophic scarring, insulin dependent diabetes mellitus, fragile skin, use of anticoagulants, epilepsy, history of coagulopathy, immunodeficiency, recent sunburn/excessive tanning, pregnancy, recent chemical or mechanical peel in treatment area (< 1 month), and pacemaker or defibrillator.

Alcohol: No

Surgical history:

No surgical history

Anxiety, Gender dysphoria, Major depressive disorder,

COVID vaccination received:

Yes
assesment:
Aesthetic - Increased Terminal Hair
Management options were discussed, including: Dermaplaning and laser hair removal.
Risks and benefits of all options were reviewed, including but not limited to erythema, edema, infection, burn, blisters, erosions, allergic reactions, delayed healing, hyperpigmentation/ hypopigmentation, scarring, recurrence of hair growth.
Patient opted to proceed with the following treatment: 
 Laser hair removal using diode laser done today. Patient is to have 4-6 treatments q 2-3 weeks apart. Patient understands that complete hair removal may not occur, but can see up to a 70% reduction in the treated areas. Patient verbalized  understanding of the risks of this procedure, including skin irritation and pigmentation changes. 
SUMMARY:
1. Laser hair removal today using diode laser.
2. Seek care with signs of infection, including swelling, wound drainage or fever.
3. Avoid sun exposure, apply SPF 30 or greater, reapplying Q 90 mins when outdoors.
4. Patient understands laser hair removal requires multiple treatments and is not permanent, a minimum of 6 sessions are recommended.
5. Patient must shave the day of treatment or the day prior.
6. Post laser instructions give to patient verbally and sent in patient packet.
7. Return to clinic in 2-3 weeks for next treatment. 
8. Apply HC 2.5% BID X1 week for burn/irritation
9. Apply aloe vera gel as needed for burn/irritation 
Follow up: 2-3 weeks
